note: Asp39 on barstar (chain D in 1BRS) is a buried interface hotspot that inserts into the barnase active-site cleft and forms polar contacts with barnase. Note barnase residue 39 is a lysine, not an aspartate; the D39A hotspot is on barstar. Alanine substitution removes the side-chain contribution and weakens binding by 3-5 orders of magnitude in Kd.
